title: Driver-race rank post-processing
summary: ROC AUC is rank-based, and pit stops occur as sparse, structured events within each driver-race trajectory.
rationale: ROC AUC is rank-based, and pit stops occur as sparse, structured events within each driver-race trajectory. F1 strategy sources frame pit stops as optimized windows, suggesting model probabilities can be improved by local ranking constraints rather than global calibration.
implementation_hint: After prediction, group by Race-Driver-Stint or Race-Driver and smooth probabilities over adjacent LapNumber with a short centered/causal window on test only, then optionally boost local maxima in plausible windows where TyreLife is high and RaceProgress is not terminal. Preserve global rank mostly by blending 80-95% raw prediction with 5-20% within-group percentile score.
expected_effect: May reduce noisy one-lap spikes and better rank the true next-lap stop within each stint, improving AUC without changing model training.
risk: Over-smoothing can hurt if the target is sharply one-lap-ahead; tune blend weight only on robust race-aware validation.
